---
title: "Part 2: 1D Smoothing and Parameter Tuning"
bibliography: ../../references.bib
---

[Back to regularizeNd series home](index.qmd)

[Previous: Part 1](part-01-introduction-and-intuition.qmd)

This part reduces the problem to 1D to make parameter effects obvious.

## Practical Goal

- Sweep smoothness values across orders of magnitude.
- Observe under-smoothing and over-smoothing.
- Build a repeatable tuning strategy.

## Example Anchor

Primary script: `regularizeNd/Examples/example1D.m`

## Suggested Walkthrough

1. Start from baseline smoothness near `1e-2`.
2. Test lower and higher values by factors of 10.
3. Plot residual behavior and curve shape together.
4. Select smoothness that balances noise rejection and trend retention.

## Practical Tuning Heuristic

When data is noisy, move smoothness up. When known structure is lost, move smoothness down.

## Attribution

This educational progression is inspired by Peter Goldstein's regularization articles and related prior tools.

## Next

[Part 3: Interpolation and Solver Behavior](part-03-interpolation-and-solver-behavior.qmd)
